goal: Arrange a concrete execution plan for the next delivery rounds on the current stack.
constraints: Do not implement code changes in this turn
acceptance_criteria: The roadmap orders work by dependency and risk
The roadmap names concrete modules or areas affected
The roadmap includes acceptance gates and verification guidance
product_acceptance_criteria: The user can use the roadmap to decide what to build next without re-planning from scratch
The roadmap clearly shows what should happen before and after procurement workflow expansion
manual_spot_checks: Check prior mature-project analysis and current optimization roadmap
Check current hotspot module sizes and concentration
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after the phased roadmap and planning artifacts are written and clearly bounded as planning-only output.
delivery_truth_contract: State only confirmed repo facts and explicit sequencing judgment; do not imply any roadmap phase is already implemented.
inferred_assumptions: The user wants an execution-ready plan, not another abstract analysis.
The next major work should deepen procurement workflow clarity and not broaden product scope dramatically.
Guardrails and seam preparation should happen before larger schema and UI moves.
